title: alkanna cappadocica
intro: Alkanna cappadocica, also known as blue alkanna, is a flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of alkanna cappadocica include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and creams, which can be applied topically to affected areas.
